learn.title: Rooftop Solar and Net Metering
learn.intro: Rooftop solar turns homes, schools, shops and factories into small power plants. In India, it reduces daytime grid demand, cuts electricity bills and supports cleaner cities. Net metering makes the idea practical: excess solar power can flow back to the distribution network, helping consumers receive credit while DISCOMs gain local generation close to where electricity is used.
learn.c1_text: Solar panels generate direct current from sunlight. An inverter converts it to alternating current for appliances. A bidirectional meter tracks both imported grid power and exported solar power. Think of it like a two-way water pipe: electricity can flow in for night use and flow out when your roof produces more than the building needs.
learn.c2_text: Net metering compares solar exported to the grid with electricity imported from it. If a household exports 5 kWh in the afternoon and imports 3 kWh at night, the net bill can credit 2 kWh, depending on state rules. Credits reduce charges, but settlement, banking periods and export limits vary by distribution company.
learn.c3_text: India promotes rooftop solar through MNRE schemes and PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana. Residential systems commonly range from 1 kW to 10 kW, while many states allow larger rooftop installations under net or gross metering. Policy details differ by state, so consumers should check their DISCOM application process, tariffs and subsidy eligibility.
learn.stat1: India rooftop solar capacity exceeded 13 GW by early 2024
learn.stat2: PM Surya Ghar targets 1 crore households for rooftop solar
learn.stat3: Central subsidy can support up to 3 kW residential systems
learn.stat4: Many Indian homes use 1 kW to 10 kW rooftop systems

learn.title: Battery Management Systems
learn.intro: Battery management systems are the quiet guardians inside modern battery packs. They monitor voltage, current and temperature so cells stay safe, last longer and deliver power efficiently. As electric vehicles, home storage and grid batteries expand, strong BMS design becomes essential for reliability, fire prevention and getting the most value from expensive battery materials.
learn.c1_text: A BMS watches each cell like a lifeguard watches swimmers. It detects overcharge, over-discharge, short circuits and overheating. If one cell in a pack reaches 4.25 volts while others are lower, the BMS can reduce charging current or disconnect the pack, preventing damage and reducing the risk of thermal runaway.
learn.c2_text: Cells age unevenly, so a BMS balances them by moving small amounts of energy between cells or bleeding excess charge through resistors. It estimates state of charge, like a fuel gauge, and state of health, which shows aging. Accurate estimates help electric vehicles display range and help storage systems bid into markets.
learn.c3_text: In electric vehicles, the BMS communicates with the charger, motor controller and thermal system. In stationary storage, it coordinates with power electronics and energy management software. India's growing EV market and falling battery costs make BMS expertise important for local manufacturing, safer batteries, longer warranties and better second-life use of retired vehicle packs.
learn.stat1: EV lithium-ion packs often contain hundreds to thousands of cells
learn.stat2: Typical cell voltage limits are around 2.5 V to 4.2 V
learn.stat3: India EV sales surpassed 1.5 million units in 2023
learn.stat4: Battery costs fell from about USD 1,400/kWh in 2010 to under USD 140/kWh in 2023

learn.title: Finding the Best Wind Sites
learn.intro: Wind resource assessment helps developers choose sites where turbines can generate clean electricity at lower cost. A good wind farm needs strong, consistent winds, suitable land access, grid connection and environmental approval. Better assessment reduces financial risk, improves energy output and supports national renewable targets by matching turbines to the wind patterns of each location.
learn.c1_text: Developers install meteorological masts or remote sensors such as LiDAR to measure wind speed, direction, turbulence and air density. Because wind power rises with the cube of wind speed, a site averaging 8 m/s can produce far more energy than one averaging 6 m/s. Small measurement errors can become big revenue differences.
learn.c2_text: Measured data is corrected for sensor height, missing records and nearby obstacles, then modelled across the project area. Developers estimate the wind speed distribution, turbine wake losses and expected annual energy production. A power curve shows how much electricity a turbine generates at each wind speed, usually from cut-in near 3 m/s to rated speed.
learn.c3_text: India has strong wind corridors in Tamil Nadu, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Rajasthan and Andhra Pradesh. Hybrid solar-wind projects are growing because wind often generates during monsoon evenings and nights, complementing daytime solar. Accurate resource assessment is crucial for repowering old turbines and securing bankable power purchase agreements.
learn.stat1: India wind installed capacity exceeded 45 GW by early 2024
learn.stat2: Power increases with wind speed cubed: 8 m/s is about 2.37 times 6 m/s
learn.stat3: Modern turbines often have hub heights above 100 metres
learn.stat4: Wind turbines commonly start generating around 3 m/s cut-in speed

learn.title: Sodium-Ion Battery Basics
learn.intro: Sodium-ion batteries are attracting attention because sodium is abundant, widely available and potentially cheaper than lithium. They may not replace lithium-ion in every device, but they could be excellent for stationary storage, low-speed electric vehicles and backup power. Affordable storage is a major missing piece for grids using more solar and wind.
learn.c1_text: Sodium sits below lithium on the periodic table and can also move between electrodes during charging and discharging. The big advantage is abundance: sodium salts are found in seawater and minerals worldwide. This may reduce supply-chain pressure, although sodium-ion cells usually have lower energy density than leading lithium-ion chemistries.
learn.c2_text: Like lithium-ion batteries, sodium-ion batteries use ions moving through an electrolyte between a negative and positive electrode. During discharge, ions travel through the electrolyte while electrons travel through the external circuit, powering devices. Sodium ions are larger and heavier, which affects electrode materials, cell size and energy density.
learn.c3_text: India needs large-scale storage to absorb midday solar, support evening peaks and improve grid reliability. Sodium-ion batteries could suit stationary systems where weight matters less than cost and safety. As Indian manufacturers scale battery production, sodium-ion technology may diversify supply chains and reduce dependence on imported critical minerals.
learn.stat1: Sodium is about 1,400 times more abundant in Earth's crust than lithium
learn.stat2: Sodium-ion cells can approach around 100 to 160 Wh/kg
learn.stat3: India announced 50 GW battery energy storage tenders by 2024
learn.stat4: Solar power can contribute over 25% of India's installed capacity

learn.title: India Renewable Purchase Obligation
learn.intro: Renewable Purchase Obligation is a policy tool that requires electricity distributors and large consumers to buy a minimum share of power from renewable sources. In India, it helps create long-term demand for solar, wind and storage, encouraging investment beyond short-term tariffs. For DISCOMs, RPO compliance links reliability, affordability and climate targets.
learn.c1_text: RPO sets a required percentage of electricity that obligated entities must procure from renewable energy. DISCOMs, open-access consumers and captive users may be covered, depending on regulation. Think of it like a nutrition label for utilities: just as a school meal must include healthy food, a DISCOM's power portfolio must include a minimum clean-energy share.
learn.c2_text: India's RPO framework can include separate trajectories for solar and non-solar renewable energy, with storage obligations emerging as variable renewables grow. Compliance can be met through power purchase agreements, renewable energy certificates or eligible renewable generation. Penalties for shortfall are intended to make the obligation credible for investors and utilities.
learn.c3_text: For DISCOMs, RPO affects procurement planning, tariff design and grid operations. Buying renewable power can lower fuel-cost exposure, but it also requires balancing evening peaks and managing contracts. Strong state electricity regulatory commissions monitor compliance, approve procurement and ensure costs are transparent for consumers while supporting national renewable energy goals.
learn.stat1: India targets 500 GW non-fossil installed capacity by 2030
learn.stat2: India aims for about 50% cumulative electric power capacity from non-fossil sources by 2030
learn.stat3: Renewable energy capacity exceeded 190 GW in India by 2024
learn.stat4: Solar RPO trajectories are set annually by India's Ministry of Power

learn.title: How Solar PV Makes Power
learn.intro: Solar photovoltaic panels turn sunlight directly into electricity without moving parts, noise or fuel combustion. This makes them ideal for rooftops, deserts, farms and floating reservoirs. Understanding the basic physics helps explain why panel angle, temperature, shading and inverter efficiency matter. Solar PV is one of the fastest-growing renewable technologies worldwide.
learn.c1_text: Sunlight is made of photons. When photons strike a silicon solar cell with enough energy, they can free electrons from atoms. The cell's internal electric field pushes these electrons in one direction, creating direct current. A useful analogy is rain hitting a waterwheel: sunlight provides the push, and the cell guides the flow.
learn.c2_text: One solar cell usually produces about 0.5 to 0.7 volts. Many cells are wired together inside a module to reach useful voltage and power. Panels generate direct current, but most buildings and grids use alternating current, so an inverter converts it. Shading one cell can reduce output unless bypass diodes help reroute current.
learn.c3_text: India's sunny climate and falling panel prices have made solar central to its energy strategy. Large solar parks, rooftop systems, agricultural feeders and floating projects all use photovoltaic modules. As solar grows, India also needs storage, flexible grids and domestic manufacturing to manage daytime generation and evening demand peaks.
learn.stat1: India solar installed capacity exceeded 80 GW by early 2024
learn.stat2: A typical residential solar panel is rated around 400 to 550 W
learn.stat3: Solar PV module efficiencies commonly range from 18% to 23%
learn.stat4: Silicon solar cells typically generate about 0.5 to 0.7 V each
